text.title: django-coding-standards Procedure
text.summary: Five-step procedure to bring a Django project to the coding standards: layout, aliased imports, service layer, keyword-only args, TextChoices, update_fields.

list.item: Action: enforce `apps/` + `core/` + `config/`. Move code from repo root or `myproject/` into the right home.
Output: layout matches the canonical tree.
Action: replace `from apps.X.models import Y` with `from apps.X import models as X_models`. Use a codemod or `templates/django_import_lint.py`.
Output: no bare cross-app symbol imports remain.
Input: views with ORM writes or external API calls in the body.
Action: move side effects into `apps/<app>/services/<topic>.py`. Views now: validate, call ONE service, return Response.
Output: views <= 25 lines; ORM writes gathered in services.
Input: service signatures with positional args; CharField status fields.
Action: add `*` separator to service signatures; convert string enums to `TextChoices` in `apps/<app>/constants.py`.
Output: every service callsite is explicit; enums are typed.
Action: replace with `.save(update_fields=[...])` listing the changed fields. Exception: creation.
Output: no bare `.save()` outside object creation paths.
